GAME CONCEPTS
If a single Attribute is used in the Check, the base TN is 15 minus the Attribute in question. character has WIL of 6  then the base TN of WIL Check for him would be (15
are used t0 represent the effects the conditions surrounding the action have on the TN:
Action Modifiers Characters can take certain special actions that affect the target numbers of their actions_ such as taking extra time t0 be careful and thorough In addition other characters can lend hand t0 help with the action_ These actions are represented by the aptly named action modifiers
two Attributes are used in the Check the base TN is 20 character with BOD of and STR minus both Attributes would have base TN of 10 for BOD/STR Check (20 101.
MODIFIERS The base TN can be adjusted depending on the difficulty of the particular task at hand and the conditions surrounding the action_ In the example of an attack test, lighting: weather and terrain factors can make the attack more difficult: If the attack- takes careful aim the attack is made easier: In any Action Check the gamemaster may choose apply modifiers to the base TN t0 reflect the current conditions_ Each modifier will raise or lower the target number depend: ing on the condition, as shown on the Target Number Modifiers Table (p. 17). Circumstances that make the action easier lower the TN_ so they are listed as negative numbers When modifi: makes the action more difficult, it is positive number that will raise the Combat Modlflers: Different   modifier tables are used specifically for ranged combat (p. 112) and melee combat (p. 113). However; they are both based on this table
SkilLS character'$ skills can have profound effect on the suC cess or failure of an Action Check: Before rolling the dice for an Action Check the player must determine if his character has a applicable skill for the task at hand. If not, the Untrained Skill Use rule applies (see below). If the character has relevant skill, then 2D10 are rolled for the Action Check; and the character'$ skill bonus is added to tne total: Do notuse the skiii bonus to modify the target number; add the skill bonus to the total from the 2010 open-ended roll. Note also that result of on the dice is fumble and an automatic failure (see p 151, even the acting character has skii bonus t0 add If more than one skill seems to apply to the task , the gamemaster must decide which is the most appropriate If they all seem equally applicable use the one with the best   skill bonus
Dlfficulty Modlflers As the name states _ the difficulty modifiers are used when the task at hand is deemed more or less difficult than usual. This is normally due to the complexity of the task_ Remember that there are other modifiers for conditions and special actions taken by the character or the target  The difficulty modifiers are used when the raw difficulty of the task is harder or easier than normal. For example, character is trying to bypass the security codes on locked vault door: The vault contains valuable mili: tary prototypes_ so the gamemaste determines that the task is Very Difficult as compared with the usual" security bypass; and assigns +4 Difficulty Modifier: The main reason difficulty modifiers are distinct from other types is because they are not used when the target number is derived from Attributes In those cases the difficulty is already based on the target's Attributes and you move on t0 the con- ditional and action modifiers_ Difficulty modifiers are applied as usual to Attribute Checks however. For example _ two characters decide settle their differ- ences in an arm-wrestling contest. The base target number for each character is equal t0 the opponent's STR BOD. No diffi- culty modifiers apply to the TN:
Untralned Skill Use character has no relevant skill to use when attempting an Action Check the character must roll more than two dice and use the worst two to determine the result: For standard skills roll 3010 ad use the lowest two dice for the result: In the case of difficult skills (shown in boldface on the Skill List; p. 63}, roll 4010 and use the lowest two dice for the result: both of the lowest dice come up as 10s_ tne player can reroll and add the results to the total as an open ended suc cess_ These extra rolls are not considered when determining which dice came up the lowest; only the original three dice (or four in the case of difficult skills) are considered Characters with exceptionally good (or bad) Attributes might have an easier (or harder) time when attempting tasks they are not trained for: This represents the concept that, all other fac tors being equal, smart person would have better chance of per forming a INT-based task than dumb person if both of them were completely untrained represent the edge that Attributes give characters untrained skill use use the untrained TN modifiers shown on the Target Number Modifiers Table_ Add together the modifiers for both of the Link Attributes for the skill question. Apply this total to the TN for the Action Check: For example character is attempting tO break the securi- code on an electronic door lock: This requires the Security Systems Skill, which she does not have. Looking at the Skill List
Condltlonal Modlfilers Ask anyone who has tried tO drive car in the snow; and tney can tell you that the conditions surrounding an action can have drastic impact on its difficulty: The conditiona modifiers
